stem: A loan application enters the loan processing pipeline. The probability of passing application review is 71%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing credit check is between 62% and 75% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing underwriting is 85%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing approval is 96%. What is the overall probability that the loan application is approved?

ground_truth.answer: P(approved) ranges from 0.359 to 0.435 as the credit check pass rate varies from 62% to 75%. Point estimate: 0.4.

solution_steps: The loan application must pass all 4 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3)×P(stage 4).
The uncertainty in the credit check stage creates a 7.6 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the credit check rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
